title: Rename browser client surface to MarkUS
description: Introduce the MarkUS product surface while preserving backwards compatibility for existing Annotate embeds. Add the markus.js browser asset/global, package metadata changes, and focused tests that prove legacy Annotate usage still works.

affected_files.reason: package metadata and publish entrypoints for MarkUS client
keep npm lock metadata aligned with package rename without running npm
track Bun lockfile generated by the repo setup path and keep package metadata reproducible
ensure browser verification web server uses Bun instead of npx for this package metadata slice

agent_notes.author: agent
agent_notes.content: Keep annotate.js as the implementation and legacy asset while markus.js acts as a small static entrypoint that forwards data-* config to annotate.js. Expose the same API object as window.MarkUS and window.Annotate to preserve existing embeds during the product rename.

metadata.reason: keep npm lock metadata aligned with package rename without running npm
track Bun lockfile generated by the repo setup path and keep package metadata reproducible
ensure browser verification web server uses Bun instead of npx for this package metadata slice

summary: Added MarkUS as the canonical browser API alias, introduced markus.js as the public asset entrypoint that forwards config to annotate.js, updated package metadata and lockfiles, switched local scripts/test server to Bun commands, and added browser coverage for MarkUS/Annotate compatibility.
